title: Executive Order 11458—Prescribing Arrangements for Developing and Coordinating a National Program For Minority Business Enterprise

flags.justification: The Secretary of Commerce's role is coordinative and promotional ('coordinate,' 'promote... mobilization,' 'establish a center for... information'), not regulatory authority over third parties.
Sec. 4 explicitly protects other agencies' existing statutory authority from being subordinated to the Secretary.
Multiple related sub-functions (coordination, information center, advisory council) all serve one coherent purpose: minority business development.
A coordination and promotion body with no binding compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing coordination function for an ongoing government priority, evergreen by nature.
Sec. 4 is an explicit, direct anti-preemption clause protecting every other agency's existing statutory functions.
A coordination and advisory function with no third-party compliance regime.

Establishes the institutional seed for what became a durable federal minority-business-development function, eventually evolving into today's Minority Business Development Agency — a genuine, significant institutional precedent.
Sec. 4's explicit protection clause is exactly the anti-conflict safeguard this flag looks for.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/14 = 7.1%. First Nixon-era order in this corpus; institutional predecessor to the Office of Minority Business Enterprise and, eventually, today's Minority Business Development Agency.
